skill.name: fix-review
skill.description: Verify fix commits address audit findings without new bugs
skill.summary: Review remediation commits against audit findings and regression risk.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Reviewed the single SKILL.md file and found no executable code, commands, scripts, or active network behavior. The hardcoded URL findings are source and resource references only, and the weak cryptographic algorithm finding on the description line is a false positive with no algorithm present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded Source URL
low_findings.description: The URL on line 4 is frontmatter metadata pointing to the upstream source repository. It does not instruct the model to make a network request or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a static source reference in YAML frontmatter. No evidence found of executable network access or data transfer.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded Resource URL
low_findings.description: The URL on line 53 is a documentation link to the source repository. It is user-facing reference text and does not create network activity by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown resource link. No evidence found that the skill performs requests or exfiltrates data.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Line 3 contains only a natural-language description of fix review. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is descriptive metadata, not code. No cryptographic operation or algorithm name is present in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Security fixes can miss root causes or introduce regressions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused validation of remediation commits.
actual_capabilities: Compares fix commits against original audit findings.
Checks whether the root cause was addressed.
Reviews changes for new bugs or vulnerabilities.
Validates that tests cover the fixed scenario.
Prompts review of similar vulnerable patterns elsewhere.
Encourages clear documentation of the resolution approach.
limitations: Does not run tests or static analysis by itself.
Requires access to the original finding and the fix commit.
Cannot prove a vulnerability is fully eliminated without project context.
Provides review guidance, not formal security certification.

use_cases.title: Validate Audit Remediation
use_cases.description: Review a developer fix against the original security finding before closing the issue.

use_cases.title: Check Pull Request Safety
use_cases.description: Confirm a pull request resolves the defect without adding new regressions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Evidence
use_cases.description: Document why a remediation is complete and which tests support the conclusion.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fix Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one fix commit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fix-review to compare this commit with the original audit finding. Tell me whether the fix addresses the issue and what evidence supports your answer.

prompt_templates.title: Regression Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fix-review on this pull request. Check whether the fix addresses the root cause, introduces side effects, and includes meaningful test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Pattern Sweep
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across related code paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fix-review to evaluate this remediation and identify similar vulnerable patterns that may still exist elsewhere in the codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Release Signoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fix-review to produce a remediation signoff summary. Include fixed behavior, remaining risk, test evidence, and any follow-up work.

output_examples.input: A commit claims to fix an authorization bypass found in an audit.
output_examples.output: The review states whether the authorization check now covers the affected path, notes missing edge-case tests, and lists residual risk.

output_examples.input: A pull request patches input validation for one endpoint.
output_examples.output: The review compares the patch to the finding, checks related endpoints, and recommends extra tests for rejected inputs.

output_examples.input: A team needs evidence before closing a remediation ticket.
output_examples.output: The result summarizes the root cause, the fix approach, validation evidence, and any unresolved assumptions.

best_practices: Provide the original audit finding, patch, and relevant tests together.
Ask for root-cause validation before asking for style feedback.
Review similar code paths before marking a finding complete.
anti_patterns: Do not close a finding because a commit mentions the ticket number.
Do not review only the changed lines when the root cause spans multiple files.
Do not treat missing tests as acceptable without a clear reason.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews commits or pull requests that claim to fix security audit findings.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides review instructions and does not include scripts or commands.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide the original finding, the fix commit, related tests, and affected code context.

faq.question: Can it find new vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can guide review for regressions and similar patterns, but it is not a full scanner.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains guidance text only and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
